page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.8xlarge is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 10 Gbps. The processor architecture is x86_64, supporting instruction set extensions such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, BMI2, and FMA.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.c5.8xlarge demonstrates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, fall into the weak category, representing the bottom 25% of tested hardware. Similarly, single-threaded gzip compression is poor, placing in the bottom 10%. However, the instance leverages its 32 dedicated physical cores to achieve average multi-core performance. Multi-threaded benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core, consistently rank in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth and database operations also perform at an average level, with Redis SET operations sustaining 23.06 million operations per second.

The hardware profile of the ecs.c5.8xlarge makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for parallelizable workloads that do not require high memory-to-CPU ratios or local SSD storage. Because it lacks local storage and exhibits weaker single-thread performance, it is less suited for single-threaded legacy applications or IOPS-heavy local database hosting. Instead, the instance is best suited for multi-threaded compute workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate machine learning inference tasks using small to medium models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 10 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark data indicates weak single-core performance, with single-threaded compression and processing tests scoring in the bottom tiers. Conversely, the instance achieves average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance, placing in the middle 50% across multi-threaded stress-ng, Geekbench, and PassMark evaluations. With a 2.0 GB per core memory ratio, the ecs.c5.8xlarge is qualitatively cost-efficient for parallelized compute tasks. It is best suited for multi-threaded workloads, static web serving, software compilation, and small-to-medium model inference.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 10 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average multi-core performance for parallel workloads.

tagline: A dedicated 32-core compute-optimized instance designed for parallelized workloads, static web serving, and multi-threaded processing without local storage.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 64.0 GB of RAM.
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and KVM hypervisor.
Average multi-core performance across CPU and memory benchmarks.
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution speeds.
Best suited for static web serving, software compilation, and parallel processing.
